teachingPoint: IR-T609-1 — T609 MarketplaceCheckoutGateway MUST validate BOLA (Broken Object Level Authorization) at ORDER 1: cart.buyerTenantId MUST equal the tenantId resolved from AsyncLocalStorage. Any other order allows a compromised tenant to checkout a cart owned by another tenant. The cart lock SETNX (checkout_lock_ttl_ms from FREEDOM) MUST follow at ORDER 2 — never before BOLA, never skipped.
positiveExample: T609 resolves tenantId from ALS at ORDER 1, compares cart.buyerTenantId === tenantId, returns DataProcessResult.failure('BOLA_VIOLATION') on mismatch. SETNX cart-lock at ORDER 2 with TTL from freedom.getConfig('checkout_lock_ttl_ms').
negativeExample: T609 applies SETNX lock at ORDER 1 before BOLA check — a cross-tenant attacker can lock another tenant's cart. Or T609 reads tenantId from request payload instead of ALS — enables tenant impersonation.
discriminatingConstraint: BOLA ORDER 1, SETNX ORDER 2 — any reorder or ALS bypass = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: BOLA ORDER 1 buyerTenantId ALS tenantId cart lock SETNX checkout_lock_ttl_ms T609 FLOW-16

teachingPoint: IR-T610-1 — T610 MarketplacePaymentSplitter MUST use SETNX(hash(tenantId+cartId+totalAmountCents)) as idempotency key — NOT cartId alone. Using cartId alone allows a retry with a different totalAmountCents to succeed twice, enabling double-charge. The hash incorporates all three fields so any field change produces a new key. On collision: unconditional return of the previously captured result.
positiveExample: T610 computes idempotencyKey = hash(tenantId + cartId + totalAmountCents). SETNX(idempotencyKey) at ORDER 1. On key exists: return cached result immediately without any payment processing.
negativeExample: T610 uses cartId as idempotency key — a retry with different totalAmountCents (fraudulent override) produces a new SETNX key and charges the buyer twice. Or T610 checks idempotency after payment capture — too late to prevent duplicate charge.
discriminatingConstraint: hash(tenantId+cartId+totalAmountCents) for SETNX key — cartId-only key = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: SETNX hash tenantId cartId totalAmountCents idempotency key T610 MarketplacePaymentSplitter double charge FLOW-16

teachingPoint: IR-T610-2 — T610 MarketplacePaymentSplitter nonRepudiationAudit index MUST be append-only (storeDocument insert only). deleteDocument or updateDocument on the nonRepudiationAudit index destroys the legal audit chain. The hash chain MUST include prevHash from the prior audit record to cryptographically link all entries. PII (card numbers, CVVs) MUST be scrubbed from all audit records before storeDocument.
positiveExample: T610 calls storeDocument(auditRecord, {appendOnly: true}) with prevHash from prior record. PII scrubbed (card data removed, only last4 retained). No deleteDocument or updateDocument calls on xiigen-nra-audit index.
negativeExample: T610 calls updateDocument to correct a previous audit entry — destroys the chain integrity. Or T610 stores raw card number in audit record — PII exposure in append-only log with no deletion path.
discriminatingConstraint: Append-only nonRepudiationAudit + prevHash chain + PII scrub — any deleteDocument/updateDocument on audit or raw PII stored = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: nonRepudiationAudit append-only storeDocument prevHash hash chain PII scrub deleteDocument prohibited T610 FLOW-16

teachingPoint: IR-T611-1 — T611 MarketplaceEscrowController MUST execute compensation in LIFO order: [REFUND_PAYMENT, RESTORE_INVENTORY]. FIFO order (RESTORE then REFUND) leaves the buyer's money captured while inventory is returned — the seller has neither money nor inventory held, creating a window for oversell. LIFO ensures money returns to buyer BEFORE inventory is released, maintaining the invariant that funds always follow goods.
positiveExample: T611 defines COMPENSATION_STEPS_LIFO = ['REFUND_PAYMENT', 'RESTORE_INVENTORY'] as const. REFUND executes at step 0, RESTORE at step 1. If REFUND fails, saga halts — RESTORE is not attempted until REFUND succeeds.
negativeExample: T611 executes RESTORE_INVENTORY then REFUND_PAYMENT (FIFO) — inventory returns to available stock before refund completes. A concurrent buyer can purchase the now-available item while the original buyer is still waiting for their refund.
discriminatingConstraint: LIFO [REFUND_PAYMENT, RESTORE_INVENTORY] — FIFO order or reversed array = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: LIFO REFUND_PAYMENT RESTORE_INVENTORY compensation order T611 MarketplaceEscrowController saga FLOW-16

teachingPoint: IR-T611-2 — T611 MarketplaceEscrowController dispute path MUST use updateDocument(status:DISPUTED, accessBlocked:true) — NEVER deleteDocument on any escrow record. Deleting escrow on dispute destroys the forensic record needed for chargeback defence. The dispute handler MUST NOT trigger the compensation saga — that is reserved for PaymentReversalRequested only. escrow_auto_release_days MUST come from FREEDOM config — not hardcoded.
positiveExample: T611.handleDisputeInitiated() calls updateDocument({status:'DISPUTED', accessBlocked:true}) and emits EscrowDisputed. No compensation saga triggered. escrow_auto_release_days read from freedom.getConfig('escrow_auto_release_days').
negativeExample: T611.handleDisputeInitiated() calls deleteDocument on escrow — destroys forensic record. Or T611 triggers LIFO compensation on dispute — premature refund before dispute is resolved.
discriminatingConstraint: updateDocument ONLY on dispute, never deleteDocument, escrow_auto_release_days from FREEDOM — hardcoded days or delete-on-dispute = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: dispute updateDocument status DISPUTED accessBlocked deleteDocument prohibited escrow_auto_release_days FREEDOM T611 FLOW-16

teachingPoint: IR-T612-1 — T612 SellerPayoutWriter MUST store only payoutVaultRef in payout records — bankAccountNumber, IBAN, routingNumber, sortCode, and accountName are PROHIBITED in any document written to xiigen-payout-records or xiigen-payout-audit. Storing bank details in a document index creates a high-value PII target. The vault reference is opaque — only the vault service can resolve it to real bank details at disbursement time.
positiveExample: T612 loads seller.payoutVaultRef from ISellerVaultService and writes { payoutVaultRef, amount, currency, orderId } to storeDocument. No bankAccountNumber, IBAN, routingNumber, or sortCode fields in any stored record.
negativeExample: T612 reads seller.bankAccountNumber from the seller profile and stores it in the payout record alongside payoutVaultRef — creates a durable PII record that cannot be deleted from the append-only payout index.
discriminatingConstraint: payoutVaultRef only — bankAccountNumber/IBAN/routingNumber/sortCode/accountName in stored record = BUILD_FAILURE.
keywords: payoutVaultRef only bankAccountNumber IBAN routingNumber sortCode prohibited payout records T612 SellerPayoutWriter FLOW-16
